title: allium humile
intro: Allium humile, also known as wild garlic or meadow garlic, is a perennial plant belonging to the Allium genus, characterized by its small, white, star-shaped flowers and onion-like scent. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Allium humile include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as added to salves and creams for topical application.
